title: Avatar
subtitle: Graphic component to represent users, entities or brands through images, letters or icons

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Displays a simple avatar loading an external image. This is the most common form of user representation.

types.title: Content types and priority
types.description: The component intelligently decides what to show based on the available data. This logic ensures something meaningful is always displayed.
types.note: Rendering priority: 1. Image (if it loads) > 2. Initials > 3. Icon > 4. Fallback (Alt letter) > 5. Default icon.

variants.title: Shape variants
variants.description: The avatar supports three geometric shapes to adapt to different design contexts.
variants.note: Use the variant input with values 'circular', 'rounded' and 'square' to change the avatar shape.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five predefined sizes that harmoniously scale the container, typography and icons.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the avatar dimension, font size for initials and icon size. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg' and 'xl'.

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: Customize the background and text color using the system palette. Ideal for differentiating users or states when no image is available.
colors.note: Use the color input with values such as 'primary', 'secondary', 'accent', 'success', 'info', 'warning' and 'danger' to change avatar colors. You can also use custom colors in rgb, hexadecimal or keyword format for full control over avatar appearance. If you use a custom color, the component will automatically calculate a contrasting text color to ensure readability of initials or the icon.

customSize.title: Custom size
customSize.description: Define exact pixel dimensions when the standard sizes do not fit. Font size and icons are calculated proportionally and automatically.
customSize.note: The customSize input overrides the size input. The internal calculation maintains initials readability.

grouped.title: Grouped avatars
grouped.description: Display multiple avatars in a single component with overlap and a display limit.
grouped.note: Use the nui-avatar-group component to group several avatars. Control the maximum visible count with max. Extra avatars are represented as a '+X' counter. You can use bordered to see a clear separation between each avatar.

error.title: Error handling (Fallback)
error.description: Automatic recovery system. If the image URL fails (404 error), the component instantly switches to showing the initials or fallback icon without breaking the layout.

inputs.title: Component inputs
inputs.description: Input properties that modify the behavior and appearance of the avatar.

alt.description: Alternative text (used to generate initials if no src)

initials.description: Initials to display (overrides those generated from alt)

color.description: Background color when no image is available (accepts theme or custom colors)

textColor.description: Text/icon color (calculated automatically if not specified)

outputs.title: Component outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the component that you can listen to in order to react to image loading errors.

imageError.description: Emitted when the image cannot be loaded. The component automatically shows the fallback.

sizes.title: Size variables
sizes.description: CSS variables that control avatar dimensions, font sizes and icon sizes.

styles.title: Style variables
styles.description: CSS variables to customize borders, default colors and typography of the avatar.

styling.title: Styles and customization
styling.description: Customize the appearance of the avatar using CSS variables. Control sizes, colors, borders and more.

tokens.title: Translation tokens
tokens.description: The nui-avatar-group component uses NuiI18nService to build the accessible label and tooltip for the excess indicator that appears when the number of avatars exceeds the max input.
tokens.note: Use the {count} placeholder in your translation string. The component replaces it at runtime with the exact number of hidden avatars.

moreProfiles.description: Accessible label and tooltip for the excess indicator (+N). Supports the {count} placeholder.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: Each avatar uses role='img' to expose itself as a semantic image. The group component wraps avatars in an accessible list structure.

roleImg.description: Identifies the avatar container as an image element for assistive technologies.

ariaLabel.description: Accessible name resolved from tooltip(), alt() or the fallback string 'avatar'.

title.description: Visual tooltip and secondary fallback accessible name for the avatar container.

imgAlt.description: Always empty ('') to prevent the inner image from being read independently; the parent's role='img' and aria-label are the accessible representation.

ariaHiddenDecorative.description: All internal decorative elements (initials, icon, fallback span) are hidden from assistive technologies.

roleList.description: The avatar group container uses role='list' so screen readers announce it as a navigable list.

roleListitem.description: Each avatar wrapper inside the group has role='listitem' to match the list context.

roleExcess.description: The excess indicator (+N) has role='img' and an internationalized aria-label so screen readers announce the hidden count.

ariaLabelExcess.description: Accessible name for the excess indicator, generated from the i18n token avatar.moreProfiles with {count} replaced.

ariaHiddenExcess.description: The visible '+N' text span is hidden from assistive technologies; only the parent container's aria-label is read.

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The accessible name resolution order ensures every avatar has a meaningful label for screen readers, even when only partially configured.
naming.note: Always provide alt or tooltip for avatars that represent real people or entities. A generic 'avatar' label gives no context to screen reader users.

tooltip.description: The tooltip input has the highest priority for the accessible name and is also rendered as the native title attribute.

alt.description: If tooltip is not present, alt is used as aria-label. It also drives automatic initials generation.

fallback.description: If neither tooltip nor alt is configured, the string 'avatar' is used as the fallback accessible name.

excess.description: The excess counter's aria-label is computed from avatar.moreProfiles, replacing {count} with the number of hidden avatars.

hidden.title: Hidden decorative elements
hidden.description: Purely decorative elements carry aria-hidden='true' to avoid duplicate or meaningless announcements by screen readers.

imgEmpty.description: The <img> element always has alt='' to prevent it from being read independently; the accessible name is on the parent container.

initials.description: The initials text is hidden; the accessible name comes from the parent container's aria-label.

icon.description: The icon element is decorative; the accessible name comes from the parent container's aria-label.

fallbackSpan.description: The fallback letter is hidden; the accessible name is provided by the parent div's aria-label.

excessSpan.description: The visible '+N' text is hidden from ATs; the parent div's aria-label provides the announcement for screen readers.

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: Set default values for all Avatar instances in your application using the provideNUI() function. Any property defined here is automatically applied to every avatar that does not override it via a direct input binding.
setup.note: Only the properties you want to override need to be defined. Properties not specified fall back to the library defaults.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: Full list of configurable properties through AvatarGlobalConfig, their types and the library defaults.

rows.color: Color theme applied to all avatars by default
rows.size: Size applied to all avatars by default
rows.variant: Visual variant applied to all avatars by default

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: Values are resolved following a strict three-level priority chain. The first matching level wins: direct input > global config > library default.
priority.note: The priority chain guarantees that components always have a valid resolved value. A direct input always wins; if not provided, the global config value is used; if not configured either, the library default is applied.
